source_intake_record.raw_question_text: At \( t = 0 \), the disk begins to rotate counterclockwise with a constant angular velocity \( \omega \) from the position shown in the figure. What is the simple harmonic motion expression of point \( P \)?

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'from the position shown in the figure'
determining the SHM phase/amplitude depends on the depicted initial position of point P
multi_step: solving requires connecting uniform circular motion to a simple harmonic motion expression
the initial phase must be inferred from the shown starting position at t=0
the answer is a full formula x = f(t), not just a label or direct visual lookup
multiple-choice options vary in amplitude, so identifying the correct expression likely combines geometric reading with physics mapping
verifiability: choices are provided, so the final answer can be matched against a finite set of expressions
the target expression is mathematically explicit and checkable for amplitude and phase
registry_passed=true with no listed asset issues
